layman_headline: Hyperliquid pulls back 11% — the strongest chart on the board is finally on sale.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a 'fear' mood right now, with most coins dipping. Hyperliquid, a high-profile decentralized exchange token, has pulled back about 11% this week after a strong run. Its chart still looks bullish with buyers stepping in on dips. This kind of pullback in an uptrend, during a fearful market, is often where swing trade opportunities emerge — buying strength on a temporary discount.
layman_action: Consider a small position in HYPE around $59-60 with a stop-loss at $55.50. If the broader market stabilizes, this could bounce back toward $67 within a week or two. Keep position size modest given the fearful market environment.
expert_reasoning: HYPE stands out with the strongest chart bias score at +1.10, showing a Bullish Marubozu candle pattern indicating pure buying conviction. The 5-day momentum is still positive (+2.2%) despite the 10.7% weekly pullback, suggesting this is a healthy retracement within an uptrend rather than a breakdown. RSI at 58.69 is in the sweet spot — not overbought, not oversold — giving room for upside. Price sits above two major moving averages confirming the moderately bullish structure. The Fear & Greed index at 27 with an improving trend (up from 25) provides a contrarian backdrop — buying quality setups during fear historically yields better risk-adjusted returns. The main headwind is BTC dominance at 56.5% pressuring alts, but HYPE's proximity to ATH (-21.6%) shows relative strength versus most alts which are down 50-90%+ from highs.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to suppress alt performance — if BTC breaks below $62K, alts including HYPE could see accelerated selling
CoinTelegraph reports BTC sagging under $62.5K on Iran strikes and US stocks pressure — geopolitical escalation could deepen the fear cycle
HYPE's tight support/resistance range ($63-$64 on chart) means a break below support could trigger a faster decline than expected
watch_list: BTC price action around the $62,000-$64,000 range — a decisive break below $62K would invalidate most alt long setups
Fear & Greed index trend — if it continues improving above 30, risk appetite could return to alts like HYPE
HYPE daily volume and whether the Bullish Marubozu pattern gets follow-through in the next 2 trading days
market_context: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' state (27/100) but improving from 25 earlier this week. BTC holds around $64K with high dominance at 56.5%, keeping alts under pressure while geopolitical tensions and regulatory uncertainty add headwinds.
context_banner: Fear is improving but alts remain under BTC dominance pressure — only the strongest chart setups merit entry, and HYPE's bullish structure with an 11% pullback offers the best risk/reward on the board.

key_signals.description: HYPE has the highest chart bias score (+1.10) with a Bullish Marubozu candle and moderately bullish MA structure, making it the standout setup among all candidates.
HYPE RSI at 58.69 sits in neutral-to-bullish territory with room to run, unlike LIT (70, overbought) or BONK (33, catching a falling knife in a bearish chart).
BTC sagging under $62.5K on Iran strikes adds near-term risk to all positions, warranting tighter stops and smaller sizing despite the bullish HYPE setup.
Fear index at 27 but improving from 25 creates a contrarian buying window — historically one of the better times to enter quality setups during moderate fear.
ETH derivatives reset narrative and DePIN/gaming rebound coverage suggest renewed institutional interest in DeFi infrastructure tokens like HYPE.
